description: To come or go into a place or space; to begin participation in. 'Koema' names the inward crossing—moving from outside to inside, from excluded to included.
sound_symbolism: The word crosses in. 'Ko' marks the threshold, entrance approached. The vowel 'e' opens to welcome. Then 'ma' grounds inside, entry complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a verb: 'mia koema' (I enter). Destination takes 'muo' (into): 'mia muo womu koema.' (I enter the house.) Opposite of 'thiru' (exit). Related to 'maewo' (threshold)—the place where entering happens.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Entering is beginning—each entry a fresh start, a new engagement, a choice to participate.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Entering conversation requires care—how we come in shapes what becomes possible.
